Title: Metabolomics and Transcriptomic Analysis Revealed the Response Mechanism of Maize to Saline-Alkali Stress

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes the isomerization of naringenin chalcone to naringenin in the flavonoid biosynthetic pathway
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant ROS scavenging and enhanced saline-alkali stress tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced by saline-alkali stress; expression increased over time and was higher in KN3894 than H146
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Root; strongly induced under 125 mM saline-alkali stress, especially in tolerant line KN3894
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Integrated metabolomics and RNA-seq/WGCNA; RT-qPCR; transient knockdown of ZmWRKY82 in maize roots followed by ZmCHI6 expression and flavonoid measurement; promoter binding/activation assays indirectly linking ZmCHI6 to regulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher ZmCHI6 expression is associated with increased flavonoid accumulation and improved saline-alkali tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Hub structural gene in the flavonoid biosynthesis module; not explicitly established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmCHI6 was identified as the hub structural gene in the MEgreen module associated with saline-alkali tolerance. 2. Its expression was significantly higher in tolerant KN3894 than in sensitive H146 under saline-alkali stress. 3. ZmCHI6 expression positively correlated with flavonoid accumulation and was activated by ZmWRKY82. 4. Suppression of ZmWRKY82 reduced ZmCHI6 expression and flavonoid content in maize roots.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate pathway gene for engineering or selecting enhanced flavonoid accumulation and saline-alkali tolerance in maize, potentially useful in combination with ZmWRKY82
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native ZmCHI6 promoter used in Y1H and dual-LUC assays; inducible by saline-alkali-responsive regulation
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Broadly targeted metabolomics; RNA-seq; WGCNA; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper also mentions other flavonoid structural genes (ZmCHS, ZmFLS, ZmF3'H), but ZmCHI6 is the experimentally validated core pathway gene directly regulated by ZmWRKY82.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: WRKY transcription factor family; W-box-binding TF
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates ZmCHI6 expression by directly binding W-box motifs in its promoter
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key saline-alkali-responsive TF with the highest connectivity in the co-expression network; promotes flavonoid accumulation and thereby improves stress tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant ROS scavenging and enhanced saline-alkali stress tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Root in maize; leaves also assessed for oxidative damage phenotypes in Arabidopsis
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Zea mays; Arabidopsis thaliana; Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: 22KN3894; H23146; atwrky51-1; atwrky51-2; ZmWRKY82-OE-2; ZmWRKY82-OE-18; INVSC1
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced by saline-alkali stress in both lines, with significantly higher expression in KN3894; expression increased with stress duration
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In maize roots, induced by saline-alkali stress; in transgenic Arabidopsis, overexpression lines show strong expression
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: ZmCHI6 promoter DNA; AtWRKY51 is a homologous factor used for comparative genetics
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 125 mM mixed saline-alkali stress (NaHCO3:Na2CO3 = 9:1, pH 9.1-9.2); yeast saline-alkali stress 1.2 M
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq/WGCNA co-expression analysis; RT-qPCR; yeast one-hybrid; dual-luciferase transient assay; EMSA; yeast stress assay; Arabidopsis overexpression and mutant analysis; maize AsODN transient silencing; flavonoid and ROS-related metabolite/physiology measurements
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes flavonoid accumulation, reduces ROS and MDA accumulation, increases antioxidant enzyme activities, and enhances saline-alkali tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmWRKY82 was identified as the top connected TF in the saline-alkali-responsive flavonoid co-expression network. 2. It directly bound the W-box motifs in the ZmCHI6 promoter and activated ZmCHI6 transcription. 3. Overexpression improved saline-alkali tolerance in yeast and Arabidopsis, whereas Arabidopsis atwrky51 mutants were more sensitive. 4. Transient silencing of ZmWRKY82 in maize reduced ZmCHI6 expression and flavonoid accumulation. 5. ZmWRKY82 enhances antioxidant capacity by promoting flavonoid biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value candidate for marker-assisted selection, transgenic engineering, or gene-editing strategies to improve maize saline-alkali tolerance through enhanced flavonoid biosynthesis; 22KN3894 is suggested as a useful donor parent
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S/overexpression vector in Arabidopsis not explicitly stated; native ZmCHI6 promoter used as reporter target in Y1H and dual-LUC assays
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Broadly targeted metabolomics; RNA-seq; WGCNA; accession NA
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Phylogenetic analysis suggested ZmWRKY82 is homologous to Arabidopsis AtWRKY51. The study functionally links WRKY-mediated transcriptional control to stress-induced flavonoid accumulation rather than describing broad downstream cascades.
